question_title: Will the interest in “bbc news” change between 2026-03-03 and 2026-03-12 according to Google Trends?
summary: The "bbc news" search interest spiked to 100 on Feb 28, 2026, driven by the US-Israel military strikes on Iran. It has been decaying rapidly since: 100→94→79→64 over 3 days. Starting at 64 on Mar 3, the question asks where it will be on Mar 12 (9 days later). The threshold for "Decreases" requires only a drop of 4+ points (below 61), which is very likely given the steep exponential decay pattern (half-life ~4.7 days projecting to ~40-50 by Mar 12). The ongoing war keeps interest elevated above the normal baseline (~32) but doesn't prevent the shock-response decay. The "Doesn't change" band (61-67) is narrow and hard to land in. Monte Carlo simulations across two models confirm Decreases as the dominant outcome at ~74%.

factors.description: Post-spike decay trajectory: BBC news interest dropped from 100 (Feb 28) to 64 (Mar 3), showing -6/-15/-15 daily decreases. Exponential decay with half-life ~4.7 days projects ~40-50 by Mar 12, well below the 61 threshold.

Active US-Israel war on Iran keeps interest elevated above normal baseline (~32) but doesn't prevent decay from 64. War expected to last 4-5 weeks, creating elevated floor but not sustaining shock-level interest.
factors.conditional: War continues without dramatic new escalation

factors.description: Historical precedent: June 2025 BBC news spike (94 weekly) dropped to 54 in one week, then to 48, 47. Initial shock search behavior decays rapidly regardless of whether underlying story continues.
Ongoing war escalations (Iran retaliating, Strait of Hormuz closed, US casualties) could generate new BBC news search spikes, potentially pushing Mar 12 value above 67.
Narrow 'Doesn't change' band (61-67, only 7 points) in a high-volatility post-spike environment makes this outcome hard to land in precisely.
Monte Carlo ensemble average across exponential-decay and war-persistence models: P(Decreases)=73.6%, P(Increases)=15.8%, P(DC)=10.6%. Median projected Mar 12 value ~49-57.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Decreases (75%), Increases (14%), Doesn't change (11%)**

Key reasoning:
- "bbc news" spiked to 100 on Feb 28 due to the US-Israel military strikes on Iran, and has been decaying steeply: 100→94→79→64 over 3 days
- Starting at 64, the "Decreases" threshold requires only a 4+ point drop (below 61) over 9 days — very achievable given the observed decay rate
- Exponential decay projects values around 40-50 by Mar 12, well into "Decreases" territory
- The ongoing war keeps interest above the ~32 baseline but doesn't prevent shock-level decay
- The "Doesn't change" band (61-67) is narrow and difficult to hit precisely in a high-volatility environment
- The main upside risk is a major new war escalation generating a BBC-specific search spike near Mar 12
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “bbc news” change between 2026-03-03 and 2026-03-12 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42408)
